US.name: United States

shortTerm.note: Can change overnight if debt demand weakens or net-selling starts

vitals.label: Debt vs income
vitals.read: Debt held by the public near one year of national income; projected to keep climbing.

vitals.label: Debt service vs income
vitals.read: Net interest to the public — cash actually leaving the government today — costs about a fifth of total revenue. Gross interest, including bonds credited to trust funds, runs higher still (see the panel below).
vitals.src: derived · Treasury (net interest, total receipts net of refunds)

terms.label: Net interest to the public
terms.src: Treasury (MTS, interest on debt held by the public)

terms.label: Total receipts, net of refunds (TTM)
terms.src: Treasury (MTS mts_table_4, total receipts net of refunds, TTM)

vitals.label: Rates vs inflation & growth
vitals.read: 10-year Treasury yield minus trailing-12-month CPI inflation — the real cost of borrowing Dalio watches.

vitals.label: Debt & service vs savings
vitals.read: Reserves including gold at market value are still thin relative to the debt — and most of the buffer is illiquid gold, not spendable FX.
vitals.src: derived · Treasury (gold oz 2026-06) + LBMA (PM fix, direct) (price 2026-07) + FRED

panels.note: Very large debt with little liquid backing. Who holds it matters: foreign holders can leave faster than domestic ones. Two debt-service rows below: net is what's actually leaving the government in cash today; gross adds interest credited to trust funds as bonds — a real claim, but not yet a cash outflow.

rows.label: Gov assets − gov debt
rows.note: ⚠ this manual figure is 509d old (dated 2025-03), past its 180d review threshold; data/manual.json needs a human to check for a newer figure

rows.label: Debt vs revenue
rows.src: derived · FYGFGDQ188S x GDP / Treasury (total receipts, net of refunds, TTM)

rows.note: CBO 10-Year Budget Projections, February 2026 baseline (FY2025–FY2036, current law) — a current-law baseline, not a prediction; changes when CBO republishes a new baseline, not necessarily when the fiscal outlook itself changes.

rows.label: Interest rate to keep debt flat (Dalio eq. #3)
rows.note: CBO 10-Year Budget Projections, February 2026 baseline (FY2025–FY2036, current law). Actual average effective rate on marketable debt held by the public (Treasury, live, 2026-06): 3.41% — gap vs. the rate required to keep debt flat: -0.8pt: the actual rate sits BELOW the stabilising threshold today — read carefully: not a settled cushion. The actual figure is an AVERAGE across all outstanding debt, including old low-coupon issues; new issuance costs more, so the average rate drifts up as the stock rolls over, closing this gap (or crossing it) over time even with no change in the required rate itself.

panels.note: The cushion a country can draw on before it must default or print. Gold is a hard-asset hedge, not spendable liquidity in the same way FX reserves are — Dalio tracks both, so both are shown here rather than picking one.

rows.label: Reserves incl. gold (market)
rows.src: derived · Treasury (gold oz 2026-06) + LBMA (PM fix, direct) (price 2026-07) + FRED

rows.label: Gold holdings, at current price
rows.src: derived · Treasury (gold oz 2026-06) + LBMA (PM fix, direct) (price 2026-07)
rows.note: Live gold oz x live gold price only — no FX-reserves or GDP denominator, so unlike the row above this one updates every time the gold price does, not just when TRESEGUSM052N/GDP themselves advance a quarter (STATUS.md §23).

rows.label: Sovereign wealth assets
rows.note: ⚠ this manual figure is 509d old (dated 2025-03), past its 180d review threshold; data/manual.json needs a human to check for a newer figure

panels.note: Economy-wide leverage and the external balance — how dependent the country is on foreign financing.

Dalio's single biggest risk mitigator. Dollar dominance across trade, debt, equities and reserves is what holds overall risk down — and what the US is undermining. Only the reserve series has long public history (IMF COFER, from 1999).

govGauge.note: Built mostly from borrowing need — including the stress case where creditors won't roll the debt over. Being able to borrow in your own currency pulls the score down.

rows.label: Current borrowing need
rows.note: TASKcbrawvalues.md §3: recomputing this live needs the MSPD maturity profile (for the rollover piece) — its endpoint is a best-effort probe (treasury.py's maturity_profile_probe()), unconfirmed from this project's dev sandbox. Stays manual until a live CI run confirms it resolves; see STATUS.md.

rows.label: Debt in own currency
rows.note: Stated constant, not a sourced manual value — the US borrows in its own currency by definition; there is no external series this could drift from, so it carries no asOf.

cbGauge.note: Income statement looks only mildly negative; the balance sheet is about as stretched as it has ever been — lots of unbacked money against tiny reserves. Reserve-currency and institutional strengths keep the total moderate.

rows.label: Central bank profitability
rows.note: Attempted live via FRED RESPPLLOPNWW ("Earnings Remittances Due to the U.S. Treasury") — see scripts/fetch.py's --verify diagnostic. A clean %-of-GDP profitability figure needs netting income against expenses in a way a single balance-sheet-liability series doesn't cleanly give without H.4.1 parsing (TASKcbrawvalues.md §2 explicitly allows reporting and stopping here rather than building a fragile scrape). Book value only.

live.note: M2 (broad money supply) / GDP — the standard measure when "money supply" is unqualified. Monetary base alone (currency + bank reserves, the central bank's own direct liability, a narrower reading of "unbacked") is 17.1% of GDP as of 2026-Q1, for comparison.

rows.label: Reserves / money
rows.note: Book gives a Z only, no raw "Reading Today" value (TASKcbrawvalues.md §2) — the live figure below is the first raw number this row has ever shown.

live.src: derived · FRED (TRESEGUSM052N + Treasury gold oz x LBMA (PM fix, direct)) / M2SL

rows.label: Currency as storehold
rows.note: The quantitative core of Dalio's storehold-of-wealth argument: a long-term cash saver has lost real value while gold gained — two components below, both live-computed against the same frozen −2.0z.

live.src: derived · FRED FEDFUNDS − CPIAUCSL YoY, full-history average
derived · LBMA (PM fix, direct), full-history CAGR

rows.label: Reserve FX / financial center
rows.note: Composite row: reserve-currency share (quantified — same figure as the "World CB reserves in USD" row) plus financial-center status, a qualitative index with no single public series, not separately measured (TASKcbrawvalues.md §2).

rows.label: Rule of law
rows.note: Index construct with no single public series — stays Z-only (TASKcbrawvalues.md §2).

live.src: derived · FRED CPIAUCSL, trailing 10y YoY stdev
live.note: Trailing 10y (since 2016-06), stdev of monthly YoY CPI inflation.

context.label: Volatility of growth (ann)
context.note: In Dalio's Ch.17 table but not separately Z-scored in our simplified 8-row model — shown as context, not a gauge row (TASKcbrawvalues.md §1).

live.src: derived · FRED GDPC1, trailing 10y YoY stdev
live.note: Trailing 10y (since 2016-Q2), stdev of quarterly YoY real GDP growth.

context.label: Long-term GDP per capita growth
context.note: In Dalio's Ch.17 table but not separately Z-scored in our simplified 8-row model — shown as context, not a gauge row (TASKcbrawvalues.md §1).

context.label: Months of reserve sales before running out
context.note: Book gives Z only, consistent with the current pace being ~zero (TASKcbrawvalues.md §2) — not added as a scored row since that Z isn't independently verifiable against the book from here; shown as live-only context.

live.note: Reserves have been flat or rising over the trailing 12 months — there is no sustained sales pace to project a runway from.

redFlags: A new round of QE to push real interest rates down.
The central government gaining control over the central bank.

provenance.revenueDefinition: TOTAL federal receipts, NET of refunds (Treasury MTS mts_table_4, current_month_net_rcpt_amt) — the ONE revenue denominator shared by all three revenue-denominated rows below (net interest, gross interest, debt/revenue). Revised 2026-07 (third time same day) after finding this pipeline had been summing GROSS receipts (before refunds), a ~7% overstatement checked against CBO's published FY2024 ($4.920T) and FY2025 ($5.235T) totals — net matched both almost exactly, gross missed both by ~7%. The prior on-budget-receipts basis is dropped: it was chosen partly for landing close to Dalio's Ch.17 22%, which the gross-receipts bug made an artifact — with the bug fixed, no basis reproduces 22% closely, so the denominator is now chosen purely on definitional grounds (the denominator every standard published interest-to-revenue figure actually uses). See STATUS.md §13.
provenance.debtServiceBasis: NET interest to the public (excl. intragovernmental GAS) / TOTAL receipts, net of refunds — headline `debt_service_to_revenue`. Net-to-public is the numerator whose scope matches debt_to_gdp (debt held by the public); gross interest incl. GAS ships as its own separate row instead. See STATUS.md §13.
provenance.grossDebtServiceBasis: GROSS interest (incl. intragovernmental Government Account Series) / TOTAL receipts, net of refunds — the explicit second debt-service row, same denominator as the net headline (see revenueDefinition).
provenance.debtToRevenueBasis: Debt held by the public ($, FYGFGDQ188S x GDP) / TOTAL receipts, net of refunds (TTM, $) — same denominator as both debt-service rows. Dalio's Ch.17 table reports only debt/GDP; his Ch.3 debt-to-revenue figure (~580%, ~700% in ten years) is a real anchor but not exactly reproduced by any tested denominator — see STATUS.md §12/§13. debt_to_gdp remains the headline.
provenance.reservesBasis: FRED reserves excl. gold + US gold holdings (Treasury, troy oz, live) x gold price, valued at MARKET not the $42.2222/oz statutory book rate. Price is live from the World Bank Commodity Markets ("Pink Sheet") — direct download, falling to a GitHub mirror of the same data if that's unreachable (TASKgoldautomation.md; replaced the previously frozen DBnomics/IMF PCPS feed, see STATUS.md §16/§21). If both World Bank legs are down, the price falls back to a hand-entered manual value (data/manual.json: goldPriceManualFallback) applied to the still-live ounce count — see STATUS.md §18 for why this replaced the prior all-manual-output fallback (which matched Dalio's Ch.17 figure by construction, not by independent computation).

fallbacksFired.reason: live IMF COFER fetch failed or exceeded its freshness threshold

provenance.cboVintage: CBO 10-Year Budget Projections, February 2026 baseline (FY2025–FY2036, current law)
